claim: Data is not a competitive moat in go-to-market tools; data is a commodity. Competitive advantage comes from platforms that get unique, unstructured data on the fly and let users derive actionable insights in real time.
actionable: Build data tools that enable users to query and enrich any data source dynamically rather than selling pre-packaged datasets; focus on making unstructured data actionable.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: Agents should assume data is inaccurate from the beginning, then provide tools for users to review, correct, and improve it over time rather than guaranteeing perfection upfront.
actionable: Design agent UX to show how results were derived (session replay), enable quick manual review of outputs, and collect user corrections to improve accuracy over time.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: Session replay showing exactly how an agent navigated to get results is P0 (highest priority) for user trust because it enables users to verify accuracy and provide feedback.
actionable: Prioritize session replay or execution trace visualization in agent products to help users understand and verify agent behavior before trusting results.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: At scale with 2 billion agent runs, unique operational challenges emerge: calculating token-based rate limits per model (not just requests/sec), managing user-provided API keys reused elsewhere, and debugging multi-step agent failures mid-execution.
actionable: For high-scale agent platforms, implement token-per-second rate limiting per model, support restart-from-checkpoint for failed multi-step runs, and provide observability for cross-tool interactions.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: The new GTM engineer role treats go-to-market activities like software engineering: as systems to analyze, test, iterate, and scale quickly, focusing on being unique rather than following standard playbooks.
actionable: Adopt systems thinking for GTM: define use cases, track metrics per feature/use case, identify expansion opportunities, and iterate quickly on winning tactics before competitors commoditize.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: You can measure agent/product success via usage distribution (which features/integrations are used most) and by categorizing customers by use case, then calculating health scores (out of N good-fit use cases, how many is each customer actually using).
actionable: Track both feature-level usage distribution and customer-level use case adoption to identify expansion opportunities and product-market fit more granularly than total usage.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: Agent platforms should be built like Figma (provide tools for users to create anything) not like ChatGPT (promise to do it for you); users want control and the ability to experiment with prompts, versions, and data sources.
actionable: Design agent/data platforms as creative tools with version control, prompt playgrounds, and data review UIs rather than as black-box automation that users must trust.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin

claim: As AI models improve over time, competitive advantage shifts from having perfect data upfront to being able to identify and execute unique tactics faster than competitors can copy them.
actionable: Build AI products that enable rapid experimentation and iteration, not ones that promise static, perfect data; the real moat is speed to execute on new insights.
source_title: How We Built it: Clay - Fireside Chat with CEO Kareem Amin
